reviews.review_msg: Very knowledgeable but hard to stay focused because his voice is really monotone.
Gary's lectures were outstanding. Grading was fair and course topics were super interesting. Loved this class and would highly recommend him as a prof.
A really good lecturer he is funny and enthusiastic - would 100% recommend! The content was fairly easy just a paper and 2 exams

Gary is an amazing prof and his lectures are very interesting! The class was super easy; 2 exams, a research paper, and participation. The exams were super easy and at the time were take-home 48-hour exams, but study for the exams by reviewing readings and lecture slides

Funniest professor I've ever had. Holy cow! I can't believe learning can be this funny and thorough simultaneously. He has seriously raised my standards for a professor. If I could give an SFU professor an Oscar, I would immediately hand it to professor McCarron.

took this course as a gpa booster. gary is an awesome prof and his lectures are interesting. i never did the weekly module readings and still did well. i 100% encourage you to go to your tutorials -- they were mandatory and your TA helps with your understanding of the content. take this course if you're a good writer! [spring 2022]

Gary is funny and engages with the students. The TAs are pretty good too!
Take this course if you're a good writer. Easy A!
His lectures are extremely engaging and interesting. He is a caring professor and his lectures are well prepared. Highly recommend this course.

Clearly very passionate about communications & philosophy which made the class so much better. It was obvious that he wasn't used to teaching online but made the lectures engaging and interesting. I knew nothing about philosophy before taking the course but by doing the readings and paying attention you will do fine! Overall a great prof!
